TCpW-11014/2/98--St.T^T.-C(H.TJ^4.^TT.H.] HI^ftcquf); â€”1JCT f * m MTOT % T H m 3 m^RT-ft ^T. 577(3T) differ 23-7-83 gRT HmRllfl faq ^ I -g^KT PlHfclRaq gKT 1. â€˘^TT.m'pT. *T. 48 (3T) TTrfhST 31-1-1984 2- W.^T.-pf. ^T. 303 (3T) TTrthST 7-6-1991 2197 GI/98 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC:. 3 (i)J MINISTRY OF HEALTH AND FAMILY WELFARE (Department of Health) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 14th August, 1998 G.S.R. 498(E).â€”Whereas the Central Government is satisfied that use of the drugs specified below are likely to involve risk to human beings and that it is necessary and expedient to prohibit the import of the said drugs in the public interest; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 10 A of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 (23 of 1940), the Central Government hereby makes the following amendment in the notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare No. G.S.R. 577(E), dated the 23rd July, 1983, namely:â€” In-the Table below to the said notification, after serial number 7 and the entry relating thereto, the following serial numbers and the entries shall be inserted, namely:â€” "8. Mepacrine Hydrocholridc (Quinacrine and its salts) in any dosage form for use for female sterilization or contraception. 9. Fenfluramine and Dexfenfluramine". [No. X-l 1014/2/98-DMS &PFA] RENU SAHNI DHAR, Jt. Secy. Foot Note,â€”The Principal notification was published in the Gazette of India vide No. GSR 577 (E), dated 23-7-1983 and subsequently amended by :â€” 1. GSRNo. 48 (E) dated 31-1-1984. 2.
